Document:
# MINOR CRIMES May be handled with civil citations.
## Public Nuisance Disruptive behavior not threatening colony safety (harassment, public obstruction, repeated frivolous complaints, disorderly behavior). - 5 min or citation
## Trespassing Entering an area without authorization. - 7 min | May become a major crime in sensitive areas (reactors, quarantine zones, armories, research areas, marshal offices, atmosphere processing, comms control, detention areas).
## Petty Theft Taking property without permission. - 8 min + return of property
## Public Intoxication Being impaired in a way that disrupts public order or safety. - 8 min or until no longer impaired
## Failure to Follow Safety Procedure Ignoring posted colony safety rules or lawful emergency instructions (ignoring airlock procedure, bypassing decontamination, mishandling industrial equipment, ignoring hazard signage). - 8 min + possible equipment confiscation
## Smoking/Substance Use in Restricted Areas In medical, industrial, command, or oxygen-sensitive areas. - 5 min
## False Statement Lying to Law Enforcement during an investigation. - 5 min (minor offense) | 10 min (major offense)
## Minor Vandalism Property damage not disrupting essential services. - 8 min + repair/compensation
## Failure to Comply with Lawful Order Ignoring a lawful order from Law Enforcement, Administration, medical, or safety personnel. - 8 min
## Breach of Colonial Contract Willful violation of a labor, housing, or settlement contract that disrupts colony operations (abandoning a contracted duty station, refusing assigned essential labor, misusing ration privileges). - 5 min | 10 min if repeated or disruptive
## Misuse of Company Equipment Using company tools, vehicles, or terminals without permission, without causing major harm. - 8 min + confiscation/access suspension
